name: Do I need a different optimization strategy for each answer engine (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, etc.)?
description: You don’t need a separate strategy for each answer engine. Focus on universal fundamentals: clear question-led answers, strong schema markup, deep topic cluster

mainEntity.name: Do I need a different optimization strategy for each answer engine (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, etc.)?

acceptedAnswer.text:
With many AI and search tools emerging, you don’t need separate strategies for each answer engine—but you do need content that’s broadly machine-readable and human-helpful. Most systems reward the same fundamentals.

Focusing on clear, question-led answers, strong authority signals, and robust schema will position you well across ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, and newer tools.

Key factors:
- Consistent Q&A structures and headings across your site
- Comprehensive schema deployment on FAQs, HowTos, and key articles
- Topic clusters that show deep expertise, not just thin coverage
- Regular testing in multiple answer engines to spot gaps

Practically, build one unified AEO strategy and validate it by manually asking your priority questions in several tools. Adjust based on where you’re not yet cited.

This keeps your efforts focused while still preparing you for a multi-engine future.
acceptedAnswer.description: You don’t need a separate strategy for each answer engine. Focus on universal fundamentals: clear question-led answers, strong schema markup, deep topic clusters, and authority signals. Then test your visibility in ChatGPT, Perplexity, and AI Overviews and refine based on where you’re not yet being cited.
